recent_calls.summary: The user called and requested to speak to a representative. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to the appropriate number successfully.

Mary Jo called to inquire about the time of a scheduled walkthrough on Thursday. The agent was unable to find the appointment in the system and offered to transfer her to a live representative, which she accepted. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent for further assistance.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called from American Shield seeking information about a dispatch and requested to speak with a live representative. The virtual assistant, Sophie, confirmed she was not a live person and proceeded to transfer the call to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred to a live representative.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 17 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user requested to speak with a real live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a human agent as requested. The call ended with a successful transfer without further conversation.

Aaron Spencer from the Center for Autism Education called to report a loose toilet that needs to be securely fastened. The agent scheduled a service appointment for the next morning at 4140 Old Mill Parkway, Saint Peters, Missouri. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another representative successfully. The call lasted 15 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user, Jacob, called to discuss a voicemail from Amy regarding verifying his presence at home for a service appointment. He confirmed he is at home and mentioned he has a free tune-up through his home warranty. The call ended abruptly before scheduling the appointment.

Paul called to schedule a summer AC checkup appointment for the week of May 11th in the afternoon. The agent offered an available slot on May 11th at 8 AM, but Paul preferred an afternoon time. The call ended abruptly after Paul repeated "Hello?" and pressed digits.

John Heitman informed the agent about a rescheduled inspection appointment due to a tornado warning and city hall closure. The agent gathered details about the inspection, confirming it was a home warranty HVAC inspection for a new installation, and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Chris called to report an issue with his central air system that is running but not cooling the house. The agent collected necessary details including service address and confirmed the information with Chris. Chris expressed interest in home maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call experience.

Russell called to update his contact information, providing his correct cell phone number to the agent. The agent confirmed the updated number and assured Russell that the information would be passed to the team. No service request was made during the call.

Steven called to change his plumbing appointment to install a new water heater on Friday, May 1st, between 8 AM and 12 PM. The agent confirmed the details and informed him that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him soon. Steven declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to reschedule a preventative maintenance appointment for their air conditioning unit originally set for tomorrow, requesting a new appointment on Wednesday mornings. The service address was updated to 1520 W Bell, Swansea, IL 62226. The user declined information about maintenance programs and did not provide feedback.
The user requested to speak to a live representative and the agent successfully transferred the call to another agent. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

Ali from American Home Shield contacted Budget to request the HVAC service report for dispatch number 34216099, with a technician visit on April 23rd. Ali requested the report be sent to their authorizations department as soon as possible, preferably the same day, and confirmed the correct service address and contact information. The call ended positively with Ali providing high feedback on the agent's assistance.

The user delivered a weather-related early dismissal announcement for Hazelwood School District. The agent then introduced herself and offered assistance, but the user ended the call immediately after.

Keisha called to reschedule her heating service appointment covered by American Home Shield. The agent confirmed the details and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. Keisha declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call experience.

The user repeatedly asked to speak with specific individuals (Stacy and Cece), but the agent was unable to connect them and kept repeating the greeting. The call ended abruptly after 29 seconds without resolution.
The call was very brief and disconnected shortly after the agent began speaking, with no interaction from the user.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed a successful call transfer.

The user, Tyrone Wilson, called to inquire about an inspection related to a replacement at a specific address but requested to speak with an operator. The agent attempted to clarify the service type but ultimately transferred the call to a live agent as requested.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

The user called to confirm if the appointment for their air conditioning service scheduled for the next day was still going ahead despite possible rain. The agent attempted to gather more details about the issue but the call ended abruptly before the user could provide further information.

The user called to arrange a replacement for a 15-year-old water heater and requested information on pricing and brands. The agent provided available appointment slots, and the user booked a service appointment for May 1st at 8 AM. The user gave positive feedback about the accuracy of the assistant and the call concluded successfully.

The user, Taylor from AutoZone, called to inquire about parts but hung up before providing details or completing the conversation. The agent attempted to engage the user but did not receive a response.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.

Tim Gaines returned the call and was greeted by the agent Sophie, who attempted to identify the purpose of the call before transferring Tim to the appropriate person. The call was transferred successfully shortly after the initial exchange.

The user, a vendor, called to speak with Jimmy but did not have a specific service address. After some clarification, the user provided a ZIP code and requested to be connected to Jimmy. The agent successfully transferred the call to the appropriate contact.

The user, Dynnell Shelton, called to schedule a regular service appointment for a water heater that is not heating and whose pilot won't light. The agent confirmed the details and scheduled the appointment for Monday morning, with a follow-up to confirm availability. The user declined to provide feedback and ended the call politely.

Michelle called to request a technician for an HVAC issue involving water leaking from the inside unit after a recent transformer problem. The agent confirmed the service address and callback number, scheduled the earliest available service appointment, and noted special access instructions. The call concluded with the user declining to provide feedback.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing herself and the user hanging up immediately after. No further conversation or assistance took place.

The user called to request a plumber to hook up a new washer and specified a time window after 6 PM on Monday due to physical therapy. The agent attempted to assist but ultimately transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The user expressed a desire to speak to a person and planned to call back Monday morning if needed.

The user called for plumbing assistance, specifically needing knobs replaced and unable to turn off the water behind the washer. The agent scheduled a service appointment for Monday and confirmed the user's request. The call had some audio issues but the service request was successfully recorded.

Kyle called to request a service visit for an air conditioning unit and identified himself as the owner. The agent attempted to gather the service address but the call was transferred to a live representative before completion.

The user, Ollie, called to schedule a furnace repair service as the furnace was not working and blowing cold air. The agent confirmed the details and offered available appointment slots, with Ollie selecting Monday, April 27th at 12 PM. Although there was a booking error, the agent assured Ollie that the request was noted and the team would follow up to confirm the appointment.

The user called Budget to inquire about replacing their home water heater, previously serviced through American Home Shield. The user expressed interest in replacing the water heater now and requested to speak with a live agent. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

Henry Gaines called to change his service request from charging the AC units to AC maintenance. The agent confirmed the details and booked an appointment for April 27, 2026, at 4 PM. The call ended with the agent asking for feedback.
The user called to schedule a service appointment for the next day and clarified that it was not an emergency. The agent began collecting the user's information but the call ended before completion.

The user called Budget and spoke briefly with agent Sophie, indicating no emergency and no specific service preference. The user requested to speak with a representative, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to cancel an appointment scheduled for March 29th at 8 AM related to heating service. The agent attempted to clarify the service details but the user mainly focused on canceling the appointment without providing additional information. The call ended with the user hanging up after confirming the cancellation request.

The user called to inquire about the status of a customer's work order related to an air conditioning system. The agent asked for details and the dispatch number, then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer.

Leanne from American Home Shield called to schedule a dispatch for an AC unit that is not cooling. The agent confirmed the dispatch details and requested appointment information, but the user corrected the customer details and requested the soonest available appointment. The call ended with the user being transferred to a live customer service representative for further assistance.
The user called seeking live customer service and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to schedule a follow-up appointment for an AC cooling issue under their American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed the details, including the service address and timing preference, and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback about the agent's attention to detail.
No conversation happened.
The user requested to speak to a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another agent who could assist further.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
